title: Cyclosorus rostratus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus rostratus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or the wood ferns. This classification places it within the order Polypodiales, a large group of ferns that are often found in a variety of habitats. Cyclosorus rostratus is also classified within the genus Cyclosorus, which is a type of fern that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The specific classification of Cyclosorus rostratus is further refined to the species level, indicating its unique characteristics and distinctions from other related species. At the highest taxonomic level, Cyclosorus rostratus is classified as a vascular plant, belonging to the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Cyclosorus rostratus, also known as the Japanese chain fern or rostrate cyclosorus, is a species of fern native to East Asia and parts of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which provide relief from pain and reduce the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus rostratus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cyclosorus rostratus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or dried powder, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or tablets to support overall health and well-being.
